title: mentzelia pumila var. integra
intro: Mentzelia pumila var. integra, also known as the low toadsucker or dwarf blazing star, is a rare and short-lived perennial plant that belongs to the family Loasaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Mentzelia pumila var. integra include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal treatment.
